title: NPR: Federal Probe Into ICE Minneapolis Killings 'Remains Elusive' Months Later
excerpt: NPR reported that months after federal immigration agents killed two U.S. citizens in Minneapolis, the status of federal criminal investigations remained opaque, with legal experts saying the government appeared to be actively preventing state accountability rather than pursuing its own.
content:
An April 10, 2026, NPR investigation found that, months after federal immigration agents killed Renee Good and Alex Pretti in Minneapolis and non-fatally shot Julio Sosa-Celis, the federal government had provided no clear public accounting of criminal investigations into the shootings — while simultaneously withholding evidence from state investigators.

University of St. Thomas law professor Rachel Moran, who studies police accountability, told NPR that federal agents appeared to not be under criminal investigation, and that the government was 'actively preventing the state from investigating.' DHS did not respond to NPR's questions about whether any officers — including those who killed Good and Pretti — faced any discipline.

  • In the Sosa-Celis case, DHS acknowledged two officers made 'untruthful statements' and placed them on administrative leave — but declined to confirm any similar scrutiny of officers in the Good or Pretti shootings.
  • Evidence the state could not access included: Good's car (shrink-wrapped in an FBI warehouse), Pretti's cell phone, and all ICE agent personnel and training records for the officers involved.
  • A federal judge ruled April 9 that the administration must produce evidence related to Good's killing within three weeks.
  • In all three shootings, federal authorities quickly claimed victims attacked agents; in all three cases, video evidence later contradicted the government's account.
Hennepin County Attorney Moriarty: 'We are not giving up. We are going to see this through.'
mediaPlaceholder: NPR audio report with interviews of Hennepin County Attorney Moriarty and law professor Rachel Moran
